Blow off top indeed. $EWY down nearly -20% in one week on the highest volume ever. The parabolic melt-up in the Kospi is such a clean example of reflexivity in real time. Several months ago the Samsung / Hynix "memory" thesis was disused by a relatively small number of people. As the move really began to accelerate and the tape "proved" the story, word started to spread, leverage poured in, and the price action itself became a fundamental. That vicious feedback loop began to feed on itself at the start of the year. Then near the peak of the move you see record extension levels, range expansion, record dollar volume, and retail pouring into leveraged products like $KORU (3x ETF). SK Hynix hit the 1,000,000 level, Samsung 1 trillion market cap level, etc. This is the "major hype" stage, when everyone can recite the same bullish narrative in my comments when I simply provide a contrarian thesis. Then all of the sudden, the unwind hits. Just as everyone thinks they know the trade, nobody actually does. Study market history enough and you'll find that capitulation and major turning points typically occur long before any signifigant shift in the fundamentals.

$USO - Completely fumbled the short oil trade. Was at the desk preparing all day yesterday for what seemed like one of the most obvious short setups of the year so far. Went to bed just before the G7 news last night and missed the first and easiest leg down off the highs at 1am. Due to some mental tilt in the morning I decided to sit out and observe before making any stupid decisions. Hope my write up and thesis on Sunday Scans yesterday helped add conviction for anyone that was looking to trade it. -32% off highs in one session is no joke. Live and learn. Onto the next

Geopolitical tensions are rising, crude oil futures just had their largest weekly gain in history, indices are finally breaking down out of their ranges, VIX is at 30, and sentiment has reached a crescendo.

I'd be lying if I said I wasn't excited for the week ahead. Very prepared and focused. Feels like some signifigant trade opportunities on the horizon. 

My big advantage right now is that I am comfortably in cash and keeping any opinions loosely held. This allows me to stay even-keeled and operate efficiently within the chaos. 

People on here get way too attached to narratives and opinions during times like these. Happens every single time. Everyone cares more to say “I told you so” vs actually performing. The irony is that nobody really knows shit.

As Soros says:

“It’s not whether you’re right or wrong that’s important, but how much money you make when you’re right and how much you lose when you’re wrong.”

“We have tested every system under the sun and, amazingly, we have found one that actually works very well. It is a very good system... The basic premise of the system is that markets move sharply when they move. If there is a sudden range expansion in a market that has been trading narrowly, human nature is to try and fade that price move. When you get a range expansion, the market is sending you a very loud, clear signal that the market is getting ready to move in the direction of that expansion.”

-Paul Tudor Jones, Market Wizards

Blow off top potential in South Korea?

While US stocks and indices have been chopping around for most of the year, the South Korean markets have been going parabolic. The KOSPI index is up 8 out of the last 9 weeks in vertical fashion thanks to A.I chip/memory euphoria.

The two big dogs SK Hynix & Samsung Electronics have a combined weighting of nearly 40% in the KOSPI composite... 

After last years move in quantum stocks and specific US single-stock ETF's, we saw just how much South Korean speculators love leverage... 

$KORU, the South Korea 3x bull ETF is up nearly 2,000% since the April 2025 lows. It traded its highest volume of the run on Friday, closing up over 15% and breaching the $500 century mark. 

Not sure how this plays out or if any opportunities arise, but all the ingredients are there for a spectacular parabolic blow off.

Very interesting observation from Christian and worth paying attention to going forward. Nasdaq attempting to stage another failed breakdown below the 20-week EMA as crypto, growth, and software are oversold. 

We had a very similar 4-month correction at the start of 2021 with two higher lows that eventually morphed into a flat top base. Growth and crypto had peaked in early 2021 and lagged the rest of the year as big cap names continued grinding higher. 

I would love a deeper correction, but it also seems that is becoming the consensus. Semis still showing no signs of stopping as $MAGS undercut and rallied at the 200-day moving average. 

If follow the 2020-21 precedent the indices can continue to move higher off this low and eventually make new highs while crypto, select growth names, and software lag, but stage big counter trend rallies

Food for thought.

I don't think the QQQ has "topped" yet for this bull cycle. IF we follow the same pattern as 2020-2022 then growth names (leading since 2023) have likely topped. QQQ/SPY will perhaps have a correction soon (or very soon) and then rally to new highs and grind higher. During that rally I would expect a lot of these beaten down former leaders to rally into flattening or declining 200dmas and set up potential short sales.

$IGV (Software), $ARKK (Growth), $BTC (Crypto) have led this this correction lower and likely going to be a good indication of where this market is headed in the short-intermediate term. All 3 groups consolidating in a tight range following a big leg down with pretty close to 1:1 correlation in price action. Do they continue to trend lower or fail to breakdown and turn up? Watching for clues.

Scrolling through some old trades from last year and came across my 13 page trade write-up before the April 7th parabolic long. Here are some notes and headlines I have saved. Read through these and you'll understand why I had so much conviction. I don’t think many people truly realized just how unprecedented that moment was...

The overnight session going into April 7th—the day the stock market bottomed:

"At current levels we are now officially experiencing the worst ever three day move on S&P futures in history"

"The % of stocks below the 40-day moving average just breached below 5%, which has only happened 5 times since 1986: Black Monday (1987), Financial Crisis (2008), Shanghai Crash (2016), Trade War (2018), Covid Crash (2020)"

Goldman Sachs - "Any bounce here won’t last -The firm warned that what started as an event-driven selloff can turn into a full-blown cyclical bear market."

Blackrock - Downgraded US stocks, says to be "cautious"

Wedbush - "Trump tariff moves are worst U.S. policy mistake since 1930"

Deutsche Bank - "We are witnessing a simultaneous collapse in the price of all US assets including equities, the dollar versus alternative reserve FX and the bond market. We are entering unchartered territory in the global financial system"

"CIRCUIT BREAKER TRIGGERED FOR TOPEX (TOKYO) FUTURES"

"NIKKEI 225 FUTURES TRADE SUSPENDED DUE TO CIRCUIT BREAKER"

"TAIWAN STOCK MARKET HALTED DOWN 10% AS TSMC & FOXCONN STOCKS PLUMMET"

"FRECNH BANKING SHARES HALTED AT OPEN"

"CHINA MARKETS PLUMMET 12% IN WORST DROP SINCE 2008 FINANCIAL CRISIS"
